payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.
High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.
High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Pocket money earned through task completion
payload.justification: Core financial entity representing the monetary value accumulated by children through task performance.

payload.description: Task assigned to children with associated earning value
payload.justification: Strategic entity representing work units that drive the earning structure.

payload.description: Earning opportunity available to children
payload.justification: Strategic representation of potential income sources visible to child users.

payload.description: Task completion progress tracked for children
payload.justification: Entity capturing the state of advancement toward task completion and earning realization.

payload.description: Weekly earnings summary for children
payload.justification: Aggregated financial entity scoped to weekly cycle for child comprehension.

payload.description: Parental oversight report for accountability
payload.justification: Information artifact supporting parental monitoring and payment decision-making.

payload.description: Longitudinal tracking record for audit purposes
payload.justification: Historical data entity enabling retrospective analysis and accountability verification.

payload.description: Weekly earnings calculation and reporting cycle
payload.justification: Temporal cycle at strategic level defining the rhythm of earnings comprehension and potentially payment.

payload.description: Child who claims earned pocket money
payload.justification: Primary beneficiary actor performing tasks and receiving earnings.

payload.description: Parent who specifies earning structures and provides oversight
payload.justification: Authority actor configuring rules and monitoring financial accountability.

payload.description: Enable children to claim earned pocket money for completed tasks
payload.justification: Direct statement of core strategic purpose articulated as an enabling goal for child users.

payload.description: Enable parents to specify task-based earning structures for children
payload.justification: Expresses parental motivation as a strategic goal to configure earning rules.

payload.description: Provide children with visibility into earning opportunities, progress, and weekly earnings
payload.justification: Articulates strategic objective of transparency and awareness for child users. | Strategic objective focused on financial comprehension at a specific cycle granularity.

payload.description: Enable parental oversight reporting for financial accountability and payment execution
payload.justification: Combines oversight and accountability as a parental strategic goal with operational consequence.

payload.description: Maintain longitudinal visibility for tracking and audit purposes
payload.justification: Strategic goal emphasizing historical record-keeping and audit capability over time.

payload.description: Define task with monetary value
payload.justification: Parent capability to define tasks represents a distinct business process

payload.description: Claim task
payload.justification: Task claiming is identified as a core business process performed by children

payload.description: View available and completed tasks
payload.justification: Viewing tasks represents a business process capability for children within current week context

payload.description: View aggregated weekly earnings
payload.justification: Viewing earnings at week end is a distinct business process for children

payload.description: View child earnings and completed tasks for payment
payload.justification: Parent viewing of child-specific data for payment purposes is a distinct business process

payload.description: Task with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Task is a core business entity that can be defined, claimed, and has monetary attributes

payload.description: Monetary value associated with task
payload.justification: Signals describe monetary values as attributes that parents assign to tasks

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregate
payload.justification: Weekly earnings represent a distinct business data object that is aggregated and viewed by children

payload.description: Completed task linkage to child actor
payload.justification: Signals explicitly describe conceptual relationship between completed tasks and children who completed them

payload.description: Historical task completion data
payload.justification: Historical completion data is identified as a business entity requiring retention and accessibility

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle
payload.justification: Signals establish weekly cycle as the fundamental temporal rhythm for task claiming, earning, and reset

payload.description: Week end occurrence for earnings view
payload.justification: Week end is identified as a specific business event when earnings become viewable

payload.description: Weekly reset returning tasks to available status
payload.justification: Weekly reset is a rule-based trigger event that changes task status automatically

payload.description: Child who claims tasks and earns pocket money
payload.justification: Signals explicitly identify children as core business actors who perform task claiming and earning activities

payload.description: Parent who defines tasks and makes payments
payload.justification: Signals describe parents performing business capabilities of task definition, viewing earnings, and making payments

payload.description: Historical task completion data must be retained and accessible
payload.justification: Requirement for retention and accessibility represents a business constraint on data lifecycle

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: Core business concept reveals fundamental goal of enabling child earning through task-based system

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation function
payload.justification: Temporal aggregation logic requirement implies a logical function that performs summation calculation over weekly boundaries.

payload.description: Task availability reset process
payload.justification: State transition logic requiring reset implies a logical process that changes task availability state at cycle boundaries.

payload.description: Historical record persistence function
payload.justification: Data retention requirement implies logical persistence mechanisms, which are functions responsible for storing data. | Data retention requirement implies logical retrieval mechanisms, which are functions responsible for accessing stored data.

payload.description: Task-to-child association rule triggered by completion event
payload.justification: Logical association requirement specifies a business rule governing when and how task-to-child relationships are established.

payload.description: Task-to-child task relationship established upon task completion
payload.justification: Signals explicitly describe a logical association requirement between task entities and child task entities, triggered by completion event. This is a clear relationship at the logical data model level.

payload.description: Weekly earnings sum calculated as temporal aggregation
payload.justification: Signals describe a derived attribute representing aggregated earnings over a weekly temporal boundary. The calculation logic implies a logical attribute of an entity (likely user or account).

payload.description: Task availability status attribute subject to temporal reset
payload.justification: Signals describe task availability as a state that undergoes transition, implying an attribute of the task entity that tracks availability status.

payload.description: Historical record entity for persistent data retention
payload.justification: Data retention requirement implies the existence of a logical entity representing historical records that must be persisted and retrieved.

payload.description: Task completion event
payload.justification: Signals explicitly reference completion event as the trigger for task-to-child relationship establishment. This is a discrete temporal event.

payload.description: Weekly cycle boundary
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference weekly boundaries as temporal cycle points for both earnings calculation and task availability reset. This represents a recurring temporal cycle.

payload.description: Weekly reset trigger for task availability state transition
payload.justification: State transition logic requiring reset at weekly boundaries implies a temporal trigger that initiates the availability reset process.

payload.description: Data retention constraint requiring historical record persistence
payload.justification: Data retention requirement represents a design constraint mandating that historical records must be retained and accessible.

payload.description: Temporal aggregation principle using weekly calculation boundaries
payload.justification: Weekly calculation boundary specification reflects a design principle governing how temporal aggregation is structured in the system.

payload.description: Event-driven relationship establishment principle
payload.justification: Task-to-child relationship triggered by completion event reflects a design principle of event-driven association logic.

payload.description: Operating system deployment node for platform compatibility
payload.justification: Signal SG951 specifies a concrete operating system version as a deployment target, representing a distinct physical node in the Builder-level infrastructure. | Signal SG952 specifies a concrete operating system version as a deployment target, representing a distinct physical node in the Builder-level infrastructure. | Signal SG953 specifies a concrete operating system version as a deployment target, representing a distinct physical node in the Builder-level infrastructure.

payload.description: Platform compatibility constraint requiring specific operating system version support
payload.justification: All three signals express normative platform compatibility requirements that constrain deployment to specific operating system versions, representing technical constraints at the Builder level.

payload.description: Automated weekly task reset event at cycle boundary
payload.justification: Signals specify automated task reset schedule triggered at weekly cycle boundaries, defining a discrete temporal event in the operational sequence.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle governing task lifecycle and reporting schedule, with automated task and allowance reset at week boundary
payload.justification: Signals establish weekly temporal boundary as the operational reporting period for earnings visibility and task claiming operations. | Signal SG975 establishes a recurring weekly cycle with specific reset behavior, representing detailed timing specification.

payload.description: iOS mobile platform deployment node with specified version requirements
payload.justification: Signal SG977 specifies iOS as a distinct named deployment target with operational constraints at the Implementer level.

payload.description: Android mobile platform deployment node with specified version requirements
payload.justification: Signal SG977 specifies Android as a distinct named deployment target with operational constraints at the Implementer level.

payload.description: Windows desktop platform deployment node with specified version requirements
payload.justification: Signal SG977 specifies Windows as a distinct named deployment target with operational constraints at the Implementer level.

payload.description: iOS platform deployment node with version constraints
payload.justification: Signals explicitly identify iOS as a distinct named deployment platform with specific version requirements. | Signals explicitly identify Android as a distinct named deployment platform with specific version requirements. | Signals explicitly identify Windows as a distinct named deployment platform with specific version requirements.

payload.description: Child user performing task claiming operations
payload.justification: Signals consistently identify children as distinct operational actors executing task claiming actions within the weekly cycle.

payload.description: Parent user configuring tasks and assigning monetary values
payload.justification: Signals consistently identify parents as distinct operational actors responsible for task configuration and value assignment functions.

payload.description: Child role with read-only access to task information and earnings data
payload.justification: Signals specify operational visibility constraints defining the child role's interface access permissions for task and earnings information.

payload.description: Parent role with authority to review task completion reports and execute/authorize payment distribution
payload.justification: Signals define parental role responsibilities for reviewing operational reports and executing payment distribution procedures. | Signal SG974 explicitly describes a parental operational role performing review and payment authorization activities at the Implementer level.

payload.description: Historical transaction and task data must be retained and accessible for audit and retrieval purposes
payload.justification: Signals mandate operational constraint requiring system to retain and provide access to historical operational data. | Signal SG976 mandates a data retention requirement as a validation constraint on system operations.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Participation
payload.description: Defines the roles, relationships, and reciprocal responsibilities of family members within the household economy, establishing who may participate and the nature of their accountability to one another.

payload.name: Earning Entitlement
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise commitment to value-based compensation, defining what constitutes earnable value, the structures through which it is earned, and the child's right to claim what has been earned.

payload.name: Performance Transparency
payload.description: Governs the enterprise obligation to provide visibility into opportunity, achievement, and outcomes, ensuring participants can see what is available, track their progress, and understand their standing.

payload.name: Parental Accountability
payload.description: Establishes parental responsibility for oversight, financial execution, and governance of the household earning system, including the obligation to monitor, report, and discharge payment commitments.

payload.name: Record Retention Obligation
payload.description: Defines the enterprise commitment to maintain historical records over time for auditability, pattern analysis, and long-term accountability across all earning and payment activities.

payload.name: Task Obligation Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining, maintaining, and making available discrete obligations that carry monetary value, governing what work is offered within the household economy and how such obligations are structured and presented to participants.

payload.name: Participation Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for governing how participants claim and fulfill obligations, establishing accountability for completion and linking performed work to the individual who executed it, ensuring proper attribution of effort within the household economy.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement Record
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and presenting the financial position resulting from completed obligations, aggregating value earned within defined cycles, and providing visibility to both earners and those responsible for disbursement to enable settlement transactions.

payload.name: Performance Record Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for preserving authoritative evidence of completed obligations across time, ensuring historical fulfillment data remains accessible for review, audit, and long-term accountability purposes beyond immediate settlement cycles.

payload.name: Operational Cycle Governance
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing temporal boundaries that govern when obligations refresh, when settlement periods conclude, and how the household economy transitions between operational states to maintain rhythm and predictability.

payload.name: Task Lifecycle State Model
payload.description: Logical design of task state transitions, availability status attributes, and completion-triggered progression to child tasks, including the event-driven relationship establishment between parent and child task entities.

payload.name: Temporal Aggregation Logic
payload.description: Logical design for accumulating task-related metrics over weekly boundaries, including the derivation logic for earnings summation, weekly cycle boundaries, and constraints governing temporal calculation windows.

payload.name: Historical Data Persistence Model
payload.description: Logical design for maintaining persistent historical records of completed transactions, including the structural model for historical entities, retention logic, and integrity constraints requiring permanent data preservation.

payload.name: Operating System Platform Compatibility
payload.description: Physical platform selection and version constraints governing deployment targets to ensure system operates within specified operating system environments and compatibility boundaries.

payload.name: Mobile Platform Deployment Specification
payload.description: Detailed deployment specifications for mobile platforms including iOS and Android deployment nodes with precise version requirements and constraints for application execution.

payload.name: User Interface Role Specification
payload.description: Detailed interface specifications defining Child and Parent user actors with precise interaction capabilities, including task claiming operations, task configuration interfaces, and role-based access control definitions for read-only and authoritative operations.

payload.name: Weekly Cycle Timing Specification
payload.description: Detailed timing specifications defining weekly operational cycle parameters including exact cycle boundary definitions, automated task reset event scheduling, task lifecycle duration constraints, and reporting schedule synchronization.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim earned pocket money for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide the capability for children to participate in the household economy by claiming earnings. Derived from the child actor role and the corresponding goal constraint.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to specify task-based earning structures for children.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to provide the capability for parents to define and govern the earning framework. Derived from the parent actor role and the corresponding goal constraint.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish tasks that carry associated earning value for children.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural relationship between tasks and earning value, foundational to the earning entitlement model.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish pocket money as compensation earned through task completion.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural relationship between pocket money and the mechanism by which it is earned.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide earning opportunities to children.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to make earning opportunities available to children.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim earned pocket money for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to support children's ability to claim what they have earned through task completion.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to specify task-based earning structures for children.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to support parental governance of earning structures through task definition.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish earning opportunities for children.
payload.rationale: What-column entity obligation: the enterprise must provide the domain entity (earning opportunity) that children interact with.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain task completion progress for children.
payload.rationale: What-column entity obligation: the enterprise is accountable for tracking the state of task completion as children progress.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide weekly earnings summaries to children.
payload.rationale: What-column entity combined with When-column cycle: the enterprise commits to produce and deliver earnings summaries on a weekly basis.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children with visibility into earning opportunities, progress, and weekly earnings.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal expressing a transparency governance constraint: the enterprise commits to ensuring visibility across three distinct information domains as a strategic obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain parental oversight reports for accountability.
payload.rationale: What-column entity CCI identifies a structural artifact (parental oversight report) that the enterprise must maintain at scope level.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish parents who specify earning structures and provide oversight.
payload.rationale: Who-column actor CCI defines the structural role and accountability of parents within the enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parental oversight reporting for financial accountability and payment execution.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal CCI expressing enterprise commitment to enable a capability (oversight reporting) for stated purposes (financial accountability, payment execution).

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain longitudinal records of all earning and payment activities for audit purposes.
payload.rationale: Both CCIs express a single enterprise obligation: to maintain historical records over time. CCI-ROW1-C-What-007 identifies the tracking record entity; CCI-ROW1-C-Why-005 provides the motivation (audit and tracking visibility). This is a governance constraint on record retention, derived from Why-column motivation content.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance complementing the actor capability, stating what the system provides at the boundary to enable task claiming.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each completed task linked to the child who completed it.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for maintaining the accountability relationship between completed work and the earning child.

payload.statement: The system shall make task definition with monetary value available to parents.
payload.rationale: System affordance enabling parents to specify earning structures through task definition.

payload.statement: A task shall be associated with a monetary value.
payload.rationale: Structural assertion that tasks carry the attribute necessary to represent earning value.

payload.statement: Pocket money shall be compensation earned through task completion.
payload.rationale: Structural definition establishing pocket money as the earned value resulting from completed task obligations.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain tasks available for claiming by children.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for stewarding the pool of earning opportunities offered to children.

payload.statement: The system shall make completed task records visible to the child who completed them.
payload.rationale: System affordance providing visibility into task completion progress at the boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings aggregate for each child.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for establishing and maintaining the settlement record aggregated within the weekly operational cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce the weekly operational cycle for task availability and earnings aggregation.
payload.rationale: Business rule governing the temporal boundaries that structure when tasks reset and when settlement periods conclude.
payload.fit_criteria: Tasks return to available status at weekly cycle boundary

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings and completed task records visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System affordance providing oversight visibility at the boundary to support accountability and payment decisions.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task completion records.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for preserving authoritative evidence of completed obligations across time for audit purposes.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce retention and accessibility of historical task completion data.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule requiring longitudinal record preservation for audit and accountability.
payload.fit_criteria: Historical task completion data remains accessible beyond immediate settlement cycles

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks and their associated monetary values available for viewing.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance expressing WHAT the system provides at the boundary (task visibility capability), not HOW it is realized. Complements the view process while remaining at Row 2 abstraction.

payload.statement: The business shall enable children to earn pocket money through completion of defined tasks.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal expressing a business-level governance constraint on the purpose and design of the task economy. This motivation constrains how the business structures the earning mechanism.

payload.statement: The system shall make completed tasks claimable by entitled children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance complementing the actor capability. Expresses the black-box capability the system provides at the boundary to enable the claim action, without specifying realisation mechanism.

payload.statement: The system shall make available and completed tasks visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance complementing the view capability. Expresses the black-box capability to present task information at the boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record linking each completed task to the child who performed it.
payload.rationale: Business-subject structural obligation derived from What-column relationship CCI. Expresses the business responsibility to maintain the attribution artefact behind the boundary, ensuring proper accountability for completed work.

payload.statement: The business shall enable children to earn pocket money through task completion.
payload.rationale: Business-subject constraint derived from Why-column goal CCI. Expresses the governance rule that the business must support the earning mechanism as a fundamental business capability.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings aggregate for each child.
payload.rationale: What-column entity declaring the business artefact that must be kept. Business-subject responsibility for maintaining the aggregate record.

payload.statement: The system shall make aggregated weekly earnings visible to the entitled child.
payload.rationale: How-column process — the black-box affordance the system provides at the boundary to enable the child's view action. System-subject capability statement (what is provided, not how it is realized).

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings and completed task records visible to the responsible parent.
payload.rationale: How-column process — the black-box affordance the system provides at the boundary to enable the parent's view action. System-subject capability statement.

payload.statement: The business shall operate on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: When-column cycle establishing the temporal governance rule for business operations. Constraint on business behaviour — the cycle boundary within which earnings aggregate and reset.

payload.statement: The business shall make earnings visible at the end of each week.
payload.rationale: When-column event triggering the business responsibility to present the earnings position. Business capability tied to a temporal event within the weekly cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of historical task completion data.
payload.rationale: What-column entity and How-column process capability together establish the business responsibility for preserving authoritative evidence of completed obligations. The What-column names the artefact; the How-column process (viewing earnings and completions) implies the artefact must be maintained to enable that capability.

payload.statement: The business shall ensure historical task completion data remains accessible for review, audit, and long-term accountability purposes.
payload.rationale: Why-column constraint establishing the governance rule for retention and accessibility of historical data beyond immediate settlement cycles. The constraint governs the business responsibility for long-term data stewardship.

payload.statement: The business shall operate on a weekly operational cycle.
payload.rationale: When-column cycle defining the temporal rhythm governing the household economy; establishes the fundamental period boundary for obligations and settlements.

payload.statement: The system shall make each child's weekly earnings visible to authorized viewers at week end.
payload.rationale: When-column event (week end occurrence) combined with What-column artefact visibility; the system provides the capability to view earnings at the temporal boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall reset all tasks to available status at the start of each weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: When-column trigger defining a business responsibility for state transition at cycle boundary; maintains the operational rhythm by returning tasks to the starting state.

payload.statement: A child can claim a task.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the child's boundary-crossing action to claim work; refines R006 by naming the actor and their real action at the system boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables the child's claim action; complementary to the actor statement, refines R006 by naming the capability the system provides at the boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record linking each completed task to the child who performed it.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to preserve the attribution relationship necessary for earned money claims; refines R006 by establishing the business artefact required for claim settlement.

payload.statement: A parent can define a task with associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the parent's boundary-crossing action to specify earning structures; refines R007 by naming the actor and their real action at the system boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall make task definition with monetary value available to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables parents to specify earning structures; complementary to the actor statement, refines R007 by naming the capability the system provides.

payload.statement: A task shall consist of a monetary value attribute.
payload.rationale: Structural statement surfaced by object-recursion on 'task with monetary value'; refines R007 by establishing the required composition of the task entity that carries earning structure.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a repository of tasks available for claim.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to steward and present work opportunities; refines R008 by establishing the business capability to make earning opportunities accessible.

payload.statement: A child can view available tasks.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the child's boundary-crossing action to view earning opportunities; refines R011 by naming the visibility capability for available work.

payload.statement: A child can view completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the child's boundary-crossing action to view progress; refines R011 by naming the visibility capability for work history.

payload.statement: A child can view their weekly earnings aggregate.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the child's boundary-crossing action to view earnings; refines R011 by naming the visibility capability for financial position within the settlement cycle.

payload.statement: The system shall present available tasks to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables opportunity visibility; complementary to the child-view-available actor statement, refines R011 by naming the system capability.

payload.statement: The system shall present completed tasks to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables progress visibility; complementary to the child-view-completed actor statement, refines R011 by naming the system capability.

payload.statement: The system shall present the weekly earnings aggregate to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables earnings visibility; complementary to the child-view-earnings actor statement, refines R011 by naming the system capability.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings aggregate for each child.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to establish and maintain the financial position artefact within settlement cycles; refines R011 by establishing the business record required for earnings visibility.

payload.statement: A parent is an actor who defines tasks with monetary value.
payload.rationale: Structural statement establishing the parent actor role and its defining capability relationship; refines R013 by recognising the actor who specifies earning structures at the boundary.

payload.statement: A parent can view child earnings and completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject statement expressing the parent's boundary-crossing action to access oversight data; refines R014 by naming the actor and their real action for financial accountability review.

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings and completed tasks visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables parental oversight; complementary to the parent-view actor statement, refines R014 by naming the system capability for accountability reporting.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task completion data.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to preserve performance evidence beyond settlement cycles; refines R014 by establishing the record retention required for long-term oversight and payment execution accountability.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly operational cycle governing task availability and earnings settlement.
payload.rationale: Constraint statement expressing the temporal governance rule that establishes rhythm and predictability; refines R008 by establishing the business rule that governs when earning opportunities refresh and settlement periods conclude.
payload.fit_criteria: Weekly cycle transitions occur at defined intervals with task status reset and earnings aggregate availability.

payload.statement: Historical task completion data shall be accessible for review and audit.
payload.rationale: Constraint statement expressing the governance rule for data accessibility; refines R014 by establishing the business rule that historical performance evidence must remain available for accountability purposes.
payload.fit_criteria: Historical data can be retrieved for any past settlement period.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical state transition that moves a task entity from Available state to Claimed state when a child actor associates with that task.
payload.rationale: Realises claimability by defining the logical state transition and actor-entity association that implements claiming at the design level.
payload.fit_criteria: Task state transitions from Available to Claimed upon child-task association establishment.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a persistent logical association between each completed task entity and the child actor entity who performed it.
payload.rationale: Realises the business record requirement by defining the logical relationship structure between task and child entities established upon completion.
payload.fit_criteria: Each completed task entity is associated with exactly one child actor entity.

payload.statement: The system shall provide a logical interface through which parent actor entities can define task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
payload.rationale: Realises availability to parents by defining the logical capability for parent actors to create and configure task entities.

payload.statement: A task entity shall comprise a monetary value attribute.
payload.rationale: Defines the logical composition of the task entity at Row 3, specifying the required monetary value attribute.
payload.fit_criteria: Every task entity instance contains a monetary value attribute.

payload.statement: The system shall derive a pocket money value from the monetary value attributes of completed task entities associated with a child actor entity.
payload.rationale: Realises the compensation relationship by defining the logical derivation function that calculates pocket money from completed task values.
payload.fit_criteria: Pocket money value equals the sum of monetary values from all completed tasks associated with the child.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a collection of task entities in Available state accessible for claim transition by child actor entities.
payload.rationale: Realises the business availability requirement by defining the logical state management for claimable tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a visibility rule that grants read access to completed task entities only to the child actor entity associated with those task entities.
payload.rationale: Realises visibility by defining the logical access boundary constraint for completed task data.
payload.fit_criteria: A child actor can access only those completed task entities with which that child has an established association.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a derived weekly earnings aggregate attribute for each child actor entity.
payload.rationale: Realises the aggregate maintenance by defining the logical derivation of the temporal aggregation attribute.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a temporal constraint that bounds all task availability state transitions and earnings aggregation calculations to weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Realises the weekly cycle enforcement by defining the logical temporal constraint on state transitions and calculations.
payload.fit_criteria: Task state resets and earnings aggregations occur only at weekly cycle boundaries.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a visibility rule that grants read access to child earnings aggregate attributes and completed task entities to parent actor entities.
payload.rationale: Realises parent visibility by defining the logical access boundary constraint for child earnings and task data.
payload.fit_criteria: Parent actors can access earnings aggregates and completed task entities for all child actors within their scope.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain persistent historical record entities for all completed task entities.
payload.rationale: Realises historical record maintenance by defining the logical persistence function for completed task data.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a data retention constraint that prohibits deletion of historical record entities for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Realises retention and accessibility enforcement by defining the logical integrity constraint on historical data.
payload.fit_criteria: No historical record entity is deleted from the logical data model.

payload.statement: The system shall provide logical read access to task entities and their monetary value attributes.
payload.rationale: Realises viewing availability by defining the logical access capability for task and monetary value data.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a derivation rule that calculates pocket money value from the monetary value attributes of task entities associated with a child actor only when those tasks have transitioned to Completed state.
payload.rationale: Realises earning through completion by defining the logical constraint that links pocket money calculation to task completion state.
payload.fit_criteria: Pocket money derivation includes only tasks in Completed state.

payload.statement: The system shall validate that a task entity is in Completed state before allowing a child actor entity to initiate a claim transition.
payload.rationale: Realises claimability of completed tasks by defining the logical validation rule for state-dependent claim transitions.
payload.fit_criteria: Claim transition is permitted only when task state is Completed.

payload.statement: A completed task entity shall maintain a logical association to exactly one child actor entity.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural relationship constraint between completed task and child entities at the logical design level.
payload.fit_criteria: Each completed task entity has cardinality of exactly one child actor entity association.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a visibility rule that grants read access to the weekly earnings aggregate attribute only to the child actor entity for whom that aggregate was derived.
payload.rationale: Realises entitled visibility by defining the logical access boundary constraint for weekly earnings data.
payload.fit_criteria: A child actor can access only the weekly earnings aggregate derived for that child.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a temporal constraint that defines all task lifecycle state transitions and earnings calculations within weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Realises the weekly cycle constraint by defining the logical temporal boundary for system operations.
payload.fit_criteria: All state transitions and calculations respect weekly cycle boundary definitions.

payload.statement: The system shall provide logical read access to earnings aggregate attributes at the end of each weekly cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: Realises end-of-week visibility by defining the logical access capability triggered by cycle boundary events.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce weekly cycle boundaries as the temporal unit for all task and earnings operations.
payload.rationale: Realises the operational cycle by defining the logical temporal constraint governing system operations.
payload.fit_criteria: All task state transitions and earnings calculations occur within discrete weekly cycle boundaries.

payload.statement: The system shall transition all task entities to Available state at the start of each weekly cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: Realises weekly reset by defining the logical state transition triggered by cycle start events.
payload.fit_criteria: All task entities have state attribute set to Available at weekly cycle start.

payload.statement: The system shall validate that a task entity is in Available state before allowing a child actor entity to initiate a claim transition.
payload.rationale: Realises the claiming capability by defining the logical validation rule for state-dependent transitions.
payload.fit_criteria: Claim transition is permitted only when task state is Available.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical state transition capability that moves a task entity from Available state to Claimed state upon child actor association.
payload.rationale: Realises claimability by defining the logical state transition mechanism at the design level.

payload.statement: The system shall establish a logical association between a completed task entity and the child actor entity who transitioned that task to Completed state.
payload.rationale: Realises record linkage by defining the logical relationship establishment process triggered by task completion.
payload.fit_criteria: Association is created between task and child entities upon task completion event.

payload.statement: The system shall provide a logical interface through which parent actor entities can create task entities and assign monetary value attributes to those entities.
payload.rationale: Realises task definition capability by defining the logical interface for parent actors to create and configure task entities.

payload.statement: The system shall provide a logical interface that enables parent actor entities to define task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
payload.rationale: Realises availability of task definition to parents by defining the logical capability interface.

payload.statement: A task entity shall comprise a monetary value attribute as a structural component.
payload.rationale: Defines the logical composition of task entity including the required monetary value attribute.
payload.fit_criteria: Every task entity contains a monetary value attribute.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical collection of task entities in Available state accessible for state transitions by child actor entities.
payload.rationale: Realises repository maintenance by defining the logical state management for available tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a visibility rule that grants read access to task entities in Available state to child actor entities.
payload.rationale: Realises viewing capability by defining the logical access boundary for available task data.
payload.fit_criteria: Child actors can access all task entities in Available state.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a visibility rule that grants read access to task entities in Completed state to the child actor entity associated with those entities.
payload.rationale: Realises completed task viewing by defining the logical access boundary constraint.
payload.fit_criteria: A child actor can access only completed task entities with which that child has an established association.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a visibility rule that grants read access to the weekly earnings aggregate attribute to the child actor entity for whom that aggregate was derived.
payload.rationale: Realises viewing of earnings by defining the logical access boundary for aggregate data.
payload.fit_criteria: A child actor can access only the weekly earnings aggregate derived for that child.

payload.statement: The system shall provide logical read access to task entities in Available state for child actor entities.
payload.rationale: Realises presentation by defining the logical access capability for available task data.

payload.statement: The system shall derive and maintain a weekly earnings aggregate attribute for each child actor entity by summing monetary value attributes from associated completed task entities within weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Realises aggregate maintenance by defining the logical derivation function with temporal boundaries.
payload.fit_criteria: Weekly earnings aggregate equals sum of monetary values from completed tasks within the current weekly cycle.

payload.statement: A parent actor entity shall be associated with the capability to create task entities and assign monetary value attributes.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural association between parent actor entity and task creation capability.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain persistent historical record entities for all completed task entities and their associated child actor relationships.
payload.rationale: Realises historical data maintenance by defining the logical persistence function for task completion data.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a data accessibility constraint that permits read access to historical record entities for review and audit purposes.
payload.rationale: Realises accessibility requirement by defining the logical access constraint for historical data.
payload.fit_criteria: Historical record entities remain accessible for read operations.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical association between a parent task entity and its child task entities.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural relationship required to support parent-child task associations at the logical design level.

payload.statement: The system shall establish the association between a task and its child tasks when the parent task transitions to completion state.
payload.rationale: Expresses the event-driven state transition logic that creates the task-to-child relationship upon completion, integrating the What relationship, How rule, When event, and Why principle.
payload.fit_criteria: Association is created if and only if the completion event occurs.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain an availability status attribute for each task entity.
payload.rationale: Defines the logical attribute required to track task availability state.

payload.statement: The system shall transition each task's availability status to its reset state on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Expresses the temporal state transition logic for availability reset, integrating the What attribute, How process, and When trigger.
payload.fit_criteria: Availability status reset occurs once per week for all applicable task instances.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical association between each weekly cycle boundary and the set of task instances whose completion timestamps fall within that boundary.
payload.rationale: The weekly cycle boundary defines the temporal partition for aggregation. This structural requirement establishes the logical relationship between the boundary entity and the task instances it encompasses.

payload.statement: The system shall derive the weekly earnings sum attribute by aggregating earnings values from all task instances associated with a given weekly cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: Combines the What-column attribute definition (weekly earnings sum) with the How-column aggregation function to express the logical derivation capability required.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce that all temporal aggregations utilize weekly calculation boundaries as the temporal unit of accumulation.
payload.rationale: The Why-column principle establishes a design-level invariant governing temporal aggregation logic, constraining the calculation window to weekly boundaries.
payload.fit_criteria: All temporal aggregation logic references weekly cycle boundaries; no aggregation logic references non-weekly temporal units.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical entity for historical records of completed transactions.
payload.rationale: Defines the logical structural model for historical data entities required to support persistent retention of completed transaction records.

payload.statement: The system shall persist historical records of completed transactions.
payload.rationale: Specifies the logical capability to store and maintain historical transaction data beyond the active transaction lifecycle.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce permanent retention of historical records once persisted.
payload.rationale: Establishes the logical integrity constraint that historical data must not be deleted or modified after persistence, ensuring compliance with data retention obligations.
payload.fit_criteria: No historical record may be deleted or altered after initial persistence; all historical records remain accessible indefinitely.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical association between each task entity and its completion state.
payload.rationale: To enable viewing of completed tasks, the system must logically model the relationship between tasks and their completion state; derived from the What/Relationship and When/Event CCIs describing task completion.
payload.fit_criteria: Each task entity in the logical model is associated with exactly one completion state value.

payload.statement: The system shall derive weekly earnings sum as a temporal aggregation of completed task values within weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: To enable viewing of child earnings, the system must logically derive weekly earnings totals using temporal aggregation logic; corresponds to the weekly earnings aggregation function and temporal aggregation principle.
payload.fit_criteria: Weekly earnings sum equals the arithmetic sum of all completed task values within the defined weekly cycle boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a historical record entity for each completed task transaction.
payload.rationale: To enable viewing of completed tasks over time, the system must logically model persistent historical records; derived from the historical record entity and persistence function CCIs.
payload.fit_criteria: Each completed task transaction has a corresponding historical record entity in the logical model.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce that historical records for completed tasks are retained permanently.
payload.rationale: To ensure completed tasks remain viewable, the system must enforce a data retention constraint preventing deletion of historical records; corresponds to the Why/Constraint CCI requiring historical record persistence.
payload.fit_criteria: No historical record entity is logically removed from the system once created.

payload.statement: The system shall provide visibility of completed task entities and derived earnings to parent role entities associated with child role entities.
payload.rationale: R060 explicitly requires the system to make earnings and tasks visible to parents; realized logically through role-based visibility rules governing access to task and earnings entities.
payload.fit_criteria: Parent role entities can query and retrieve completed task entities and weekly earnings sum for their associated child role entities.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical association between parent role entities and child role entities.
payload.rationale: To determine which child earnings and tasks are visible to which parent, the system must logically model the parent-child role relationship; implied by R060's requirement for parent-specific visibility.
payload.fit_criteria: Each parent role entity is associated with one or more child role entities in the logical model.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a logical association between child role entities and task entities.
payload.rationale: To provide visibility of child-specific tasks to parents, the system must logically model which tasks belong to which child; implied by R060's requirement for viewing child tasks.
payload.fit_criteria: Each task entity is associated with exactly one child role entity in the logical model.

payload.statement: The system shall persist task state transitions in a relational database supporting Available and Claimed state values.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of Row 3 state transition logic requires a persistence mechanism and concrete state schema.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a relational foreign key association between completed task records and child actor records.
payload.rationale: Physical schema structure implementing the logical association requires a foreign key relationship.

payload.statement: The system shall provide a REST API interface through which parent actors can create task records with monetary value fields.
payload.rationale: Physical interface realisation specifies REST as the integration technology for parent task definition operations.

payload.statement: The task record schema shall include a monetary value field of numeric type.
payload.rationale: Physical data schema must contain a concrete field to store the monetary value attribute.

payload.statement: The system shall calculate pocket money totals using an aggregation function over monetary value fields of completed task records associated with a child actor.
payload.rationale: Physical realisation of earnings derivation requires a concrete aggregation mechanism over persisted monetary fields.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a query-accessible collection of task records filtered by Available state value.
payload.rationale: Physical access to available tasks requires a query mechanism with state-based filtering capability.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce row-level access control rules restricting read access to completed task records based on associated child actor identity.
payload.rationale: Physical implementation of visibility constraint requires database or application-layer access control mechanisms.

payload.statement: The system shall persist a derived weekly earnings aggregate field for each child actor record.
payload.rationale: Physical storage of the computed weekly total requires a dedicated field in the child actor schema.

payload.statement: The system shall execute all task state transitions and earnings aggregations using weekly time window boundaries defined in UTC.
payload.rationale: Physical temporal constraint requires concrete time zone specification and windowing implementation.
payload.fit_criteria: All operations bounded to calendar week starting Monday 00:00:00 UTC

payload.statement: The system shall enforce access control rules granting parent actors read access to child earnings aggregate fields and completed task records.
payload.rationale: Physical access control implementation must support parent role visibility permissions.

payload.statement: The system shall persist completed task records to a historical archive table or partition.
payload.rationale: Physical historical record storage requires a dedicated archive structure within the database.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a database constraint prohibiting DELETE operations on historical task records.
payload.rationale: Physical retention enforcement requires database-level or application-layer delete prevention.
payload.fit_criteria: Zero historical records deleted after persistence

payload.statement: The system shall provide a REST API endpoint supporting read operations on task records and their monetary value fields.
payload.rationale: Physical read interface requires a concrete API endpoint exposing task data.

payload.statement: The system shall filter task records by Completed state value before including their monetary values in pocket money calculations.
payload.rationale: Physical calculation rule requires a state-based filter in the aggregation query or function.
payload.fit_criteria: Only tasks with state='Completed' included in sum

payload.statement: The system shall validate task state field value equals Completed before processing a child actor claim transition request.
payload.rationale: Physical pre-condition check requires a state field comparison in the claim handler.

payload.statement: The completed task record schema shall enforce a unique foreign key constraint referencing exactly one child actor record.
payload.rationale: Physical cardinality enforcement requires a database foreign key constraint with uniqueness.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce row-level access control restricting read access to weekly earnings aggregate fields based on owning child actor identity.
payload.rationale: Physical visibility constraint requires access control filtering on earnings data retrieval.

payload.statement: The system shall implement weekly cycle boundaries using a time-series partitioning or windowing mechanism aligned to calendar weeks.
payload.rationale: Physical temporal boundary implementation requires partitioning or windowing infrastructure.
payload.fit_criteria: All operations partition data by ISO week number

payload.statement: The system shall provide a REST API endpoint exposing earnings aggregate fields queryable at weekly boundary timestamps.
payload.rationale: Physical read access to time-bounded aggregates requires a query interface with temporal parameters.

payload.statement: The system shall implement all task lifecycle operations and earnings calculations using weekly time windows as the temporal aggregation unit.
payload.rationale: Physical temporal unit constraint requires consistent windowing infrastructure across all operations.
payload.fit_criteria: All aggregations use 7-day windows aligned to week start

payload.statement: The system shall execute a scheduled batch process at each weekly boundary start to update all task state fields to Available.
payload.rationale: Physical weekly reset requires a scheduled job or trigger executing state updates at boundary times.

payload.statement: The system shall validate task state field value equals Available before processing a child actor claim transition request.
payload.rationale: Physical pre-condition validation requires a state field check in the claim API handler.

payload.statement: The system shall execute a database update transaction changing task state from Available to Claimed and associating the child actor foreign key.
payload.rationale: Physical state transition requires an atomic transaction updating state and relationship fields.

payload.statement: The system shall persist the child actor foreign key reference in the completed task record upon Completed state transition.
payload.rationale: Physical association creation requires writing the foreign key value during completion transaction.

payload.statement: The system shall provide a REST API endpoint accepting task creation requests with monetary value parameters from authenticated parent actors.
payload.rationale: Physical parent interface requires a concrete API endpoint with authentication and parameter handling.

payload.statement: The system shall provide a REST API endpoint accepting task entity creation requests with monetary value fields from parent actor clients.
payload.rationale: Physical task definition interface requires a concrete API endpoint supporting creation with value assignment.

payload.statement: The task record physical schema shall include a monetary value column.
payload.rationale: Physical structural requirement specifies the inclusion of a value field in the task table schema.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain an indexed query capability filtering task records by Available state value for child actor access.
payload.rationale: Physical collection access requires query indexing or filtering on state for performance and correctness.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce access control rules granting child actors read access to task records filtered by Available state value.
payload.rationale: Physical visibility rule requires state-filtered authorization in the task query API.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce row-level access control granting child actors read access only to completed task records where the child actor foreign key matches the authenticated actor identity.
payload.rationale: Physical ownership-based access control requires foreign key matching in query authorization.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce access control rules restricting read access to weekly earnings aggregate fields based on owning child actor identity match.
payload.rationale: Physical enforcement of earnings visibility requires identity-based filtering on aggregate field access.

payload.statement: The system shall provide a REST API endpoint returning task records filtered by Available state for authenticated child actor requests.
payload.rationale: Physical read interface requires a concrete API endpoint with state filtering and authentication.

payload.statement: The system shall execute a scheduled aggregation function summing monetary value fields from completed task records within weekly time windows for each child actor.
payload.rationale: Physical earnings calculation requires a scheduled aggregation job operating over time-windowed data.

payload.statement: The parent actor entity schema shall include authorization attributes enabling task creation and monetary value assignment operations.
payload.rationale: Physical capability association requires role or permission attributes in the parent actor record schema.

payload.statement: The system shall persist completed task records with child actor foreign key relationships to a historical archive table.
payload.rationale: Physical historical record storage requires archival persistence including relationship data.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce read-only access permissions on historical record tables for audit and review operations.
payload.rationale: Physical access constraint requires database or application-layer read-only permission enforcement on archive tables.

payload.statement: The task record schema shall include a parent task foreign key field supporting hierarchical task relationships.
payload.rationale: Physical parent-child task association requires a self-referencing foreign key in the task table.

payload.statement: The system shall execute a database transaction populating child task foreign key references when the parent task state field transitions to Completed.
payload.rationale: Physical association creation requires a transactional update triggered by parent state change.

payload.statement: The task record schema shall include an availability status field.
payload.rationale: Physical attribute storage requires a dedicated status column in the task table.

payload.statement: The system shall execute a scheduled job updating task availability status fields to reset values at weekly cycle boundaries.
payload.rationale: Physical weekly reset requires a time-triggered batch process performing status updates.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a time-series index associating task completion timestamp fields with weekly partition keys.
payload.rationale: Physical temporal association requires time-based partitioning or indexing infrastructure.

payload.statement: The system shall execute an aggregation query summing earnings value fields from task records grouped by weekly partition key.
payload.rationale: Physical earnings derivation requires a SQL or equivalent aggregation function with temporal grouping.

payload.statement: The system shall implement all temporal aggregation queries using weekly time window boundaries as the GROUP BY unit.
payload.rationale: Physical aggregation constraint requires consistent time window implementation across all queries.
payload.fit_criteria: All aggregations GROUP BY ISO week

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a historical transaction record table schema.
payload.rationale: Physical historical entity requires a dedicated table structure for transaction archives.

payload.statement: The system shall execute INSERT operations writing completed transaction records to the historical transaction table.
payload.rationale: Physical persistence of historical data requires insert transactions to the archive table.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce a database trigger or constraint preventing DELETE operations on the historical transaction table.
payload.rationale: Physical retention enforcement requires database-level delete prevention mechanisms.
payload.fit_criteria: Zero records deleted from historical table

payload.statement: The task record schema shall include a completion state field.
payload.rationale: Physical association between task and completion requires a state field in the task schema.

payload.statement: The system shall execute a time-windowed aggregation query summing completed task value fields partitioned by weekly boundaries.
payload.rationale: Physical earnings derivation requires a temporal aggregation with weekly partitioning.

payload.statement: The system shall maintain a historical transaction record schema with fields for completed task attributes.
payload.rationale: Physical historical entity requires a table schema capturing transaction-level task data.

payload.statement: The system shall enforce permanent retention by disabling DELETE and TRUNCATE operations on completed task historical records.
payload.rationale: Physical retention constraint requires database permission or trigger-based delete prevention.
payload.fit_criteria: No deletion operations permitted on historical tables

payload.statement: The system shall provide a REST API endpoint granting parent actors read access to completed task records and earnings aggregate fields associated with their child actors.
payload.rationale: Physical visibility interface requires a parent-accessible API exposing child task and earnings data.

payload.statement: The system schema shall include a foreign key relationship between parent actor records and child actor records.
payload.rationale: Physical parent-child association requires a database foreign key in the child actor table.

payload.statement: The task record schema shall include a foreign key field referencing child actor records.
payload.rationale: Physical association between child and task requires a foreign key column in the task table.

payload.statement: The system shall be deployed on operating system nodes that satisfy platform compatibility requirements.
payload.rationale: Establishes the physical deployment target obligation for the system to operate on compatible OS nodes.

payload.statement: The system shall support only those operating system versions that meet the specified platform compatibility constraints.
payload.rationale: Imposes a normative restriction on permissible OS versions, derived from the Why-column platform compatibility constraint.

payload.description: Establishes the core purpose of enabling children to claim earned pocket money for completed tasks.
Defines parental motivation to specify task-based earning structure for children.
Expresses the goal of providing children visibility into earning opportunities and their progress.
Captures the objective of enabling children to understand their weekly earnings.
Articulates parental need for oversight reporting to support financial accountability and payment.
Indicates the purpose of maintaining longitudinal visibility for tracking or audit purposes.
Section header indicating the following content describes the system at a high conceptual level.
Core business concept establishing children as actors who claim tasks and earn pocket money over weekly cycles.
Business capability for parents to define tasks and associated monetary values.
Conceptual relationship establishing linkage between completed tasks and child actors.
Business capability for children to view both available and personally completed tasks within the current week.
Business capability for children to view aggregated weekly earnings at week end.
Business capability for parents to view child-specific earnings and completed tasks for payment purposes.
Business rule defining weekly reset cycle where all tasks return to available status.
Business requirement for retention and accessibility of historical task completion data.

Business capability for children to view aggregated weekly earnings at week end.
Business capability for parents to view child-specific earnings and completed tasks for payment purposes.
Business rule defining weekly reset cycle where all tasks return to available status.
Business requirement for retention and accessibility of historical task completion data.
Logical association requirement specifying task-to-child relationship upon completion event.
Temporal aggregation logic requirement specifying weekly calculation boundary for earnings summation.
Temporal state transition logic requiring task availability reset at weekly cycle boundary.
Data retention requirement implying logical persistence and retrieval mechanisms for historical records.

Identifies children as operational actors performing task claiming actions throughout a weekly operational cycle.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment.
Specifies operational visibility requirements for children as system users accessing task information.
Defines weekly temporal boundary as operational reporting schedule for children viewing earnings.
Specifies parental operational role in reviewing reports to execute payment distribution procedures.
Establishes weekly operational cycle with automated task reset schedule at week boundaries.
Mandates operational requirement for historical data retention and access capability.
Specifies operational deployment platform constraints across iOS, Android, and Windows environments with version requirements.
